title: Chemical, biological & physical scientists
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments scientific work through accelerated data analysis, literature synthesis, and predictive modeling in R&D, which is explicitly noted as a key skill in demand. However, substitution remains low because the core role requires physical laboratory experimentation, adherence to strict regulatory quality standards, and high-level human judgment in complex problem-solving.

title: Environmental/conservation specialists
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through advanced environmental modeling, data analysis for circular lifecycles, and automated technical report writing, which are highlighted as key skills in demand. However, substitution remains low because the core work requires physical site inspections, regulatory oversight, and complex human judgment in waste management and environmental health.

title: Health and safety officers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments health and safety officers by automating risk assessment documentation, monitoring real-time sensor data for hazards, and drafting compliance reports. However, the role remains largely non-substitutable due to the requirement for physical site inspections, legal accountability, and the complex human judgment needed to manage workplace safety culture and emergency response.

title: Other engineering professionals
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments engineering design, simulation, and predictive maintenance, allowing professionals to iterate faster and optimize complex systems. However, the core of the role involves physical site presence, regulatory accountability, and complex problem-solving in the material world, which limits the potential for full end-to-end substitution.

title: Production managers in manufacturing
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments production managers through predictive maintenance, supply chain optimization, and real-time resource allocation, greatly increasing their operational oversight. However, the role remains low in substitutability because it requires physical presence in manufacturing facilities, complex human leadership, and accountability for safety and regulatory compliance in high-stakes environments like biopharma.

title: Production, design & QC engineers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through automated quality monitoring, generative design for engineering prototypes, and predictive maintenance modeling. However, substitution remains low because these professionals operate in highly regulated environments (biopharma, medical devices) requiring human accountability, physical site inspections, and complex cross-functional leadership.

title: Science & engineering technicians
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through automated data analysis, quality control monitoring, and technical report writing, which are specifically mentioned as in-demand skills. However, the core of the work remains highly physical and site-dependent, requiring laboratory manipulation, equipment maintenance, and adherence to strict regulatory standards that prevent full substitution.

title: ICT professionals n.e.c.
exposure_rationale: This group includes architects, data scientists, and security engineers who see massive productivity gains from AI in code generation, threat detection, and data modeling. While AI can automate routine tasks like basic web design or initial data cleaning, the core responsibilities of system architecture, cybersecurity strategy, and complex business analysis require high-level human judgment and accountability.

title: ICT specialist & project managers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments ICT managers by automating project tracking, resource allocation, and technical documentation, allowing them to oversee more complex portfolios. However, the core of the role involves high-level strategic decision-making, stakeholder management, and navigating complex human and regulatory constraints, which remain difficult for AI to substitute.

title: IT technicians
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments IT technicians by automating diagnostic scripts, log analysis, and drafting troubleshooting guides, which increases their individual throughput. However, the core economic activity of first-line user support and routine operations is highly substitutable by AI-driven self-service portals and automated ticketing systems, though a human remains necessary for physical hardware issues and complex on-site interventions.

title: Programmers & software developers
exposure_rationale: AI tools like LLM-based coding assistants provide massive productivity gains in code generation, debugging, and documentation, making it a premier example of augmentation. While entry-level tasks and routine scripting are increasingly substitutable, the core responsibilities of system architecture, security, and complex problem-solving still require high-level human oversight and judgment.

title: Accountants & tax experts
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments productivity in tax research, report drafting, and complex data visualization (e.g., Power BI), which are core to modern accounting. However, routine tasks like reconciliation, basic auditing, and bookkeeping are highly substitutable, leading to the long-term employment decline noted in the SOLAS report, even as high-level advisory and regulatory roles remain human-led.

title: Actuaries, economists & statisticians; other business professionals
exposure_rationale: These roles are highly complementary as AI significantly accelerates complex data modeling, statistical analysis, and economic forecasting, allowing professionals to focus on high-level interpretation and strategy. While AI can automate routine data processing and basic report generation (substitutable), the core value of these roles relies on regulated judgment, ethical oversight, and communicating complex risks to stakeholders, which remains a human-led activity.

title: Financial accounts managers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating complex data reconciliation, fraud detection, and financial reporting, allowing managers to focus on high-level strategy and compliance. However, while routine accounting and data-heavy tasks are highly substitutable, the core of the role requires human oversight for regulatory accountability and managing complex stakeholder relationships.

title: Financial administrative occupations
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating complex reconciliations, payroll calculations, and credit risk modeling, allowing workers to focus on exceptions and compliance. However, the core economic activity—data entry, routine bookkeeping, and basic transaction processing—is highly susceptible to end-to-end automation through RPA and LLM-based document processing, leading to high substitution risk for entry-level administrative tasks.

title: Financial analysts & insurance underwriters
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating complex data synthesis, risk modeling, and ESG analysis, allowing analysts to focus on high-level strategy and client advisory. However, routine underwriting and basic financial reporting are highly substitutable as AI models can increasingly handle automated risk assessment and fraud detection without human intervention.

title: Financial managers & directors
exposure_rationale: Financial managers and directors have high complementary exposure as AI tools significantly enhance their ability to perform complex data analysis, fraud detection, and strategic forecasting. However, their substitutable exposure remains low because the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, regulatory accountability, and the management of complex stakeholder relationships that cannot be fully automated.

title: HR managers & officers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments HR by automating recruitment screening, drafting performance reviews, and analyzing employee engagement data, allowing managers to focus on high-value strategy. However, the core of the role involves industrial relations, employee welfare, and complex human judgment, which require a level of trust and interpersonal nuance that AI cannot fully substitute.

title: Mgt. consultants, business analysts & project managers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating data synthesis, drafting project plans, and performing complex business analysis, which increases the throughput of consultants and managers. However, the core of the role relies on human-centric leadership, stakeholder management, and navigating organizational politics, which remain difficult for AI to substitute entirely.

title: Other business associate professionals
exposure_rationale: This group includes data analysts and accounting technicians who benefit significantly from AI-driven data visualization and automated reconciliation, boosting their productivity. However, many core tasks like basic financial reporting, estimating, and data annotation are highly susceptible to full automation by current AI technologies.

title: Health associate professionals
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through automated diagnostic imaging analysis, pharmaceutical dispensing accuracy, and real-time patient monitoring data. However, the core tasks—including physical paramedic intervention, dental nursing, and hands-on technical procedures—require physical presence and human accountability that AI cannot substitute.

title: Medical practitioners
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments medical practice through enhanced differential diagnosis, medical imaging analysis, and the automation of clinical documentation. However, the core of the role remains highly insulated from substitution due to the requirement for physical examinations, complex surgical procedures, and the high-stakes human trust and ethical judgment inherent in patient care.

title: Nurses & midwives
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments nursing through enhanced patient monitoring, automated charting, and diagnostic support, which increases productivity in a sector facing severe labor shortages. However, the core of the role involves physical care, clinical procedures, and empathetic human interaction that AI cannot substitute, especially in highly regulated and trust-based healthcare environments.

title: Other healthcare professionals
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through enhanced medical imaging analysis for radiographers, drug interaction checks for pharmacists, and diagnostic support for dental and medical practitioners. However, substitution remains very low because the core of these professions involves physical procedures, regulated clinical accountability, and high-trust human interactions that cannot be automated.

title: Therapy professionals
exposure_rationale: AI provides significant complementary value through automated clinical documentation, personalized exercise/rehabilitation plan generation, and diagnostic support for speech or movement patterns. However, substitution is extremely low because these roles require physical manipulation, sensory observation, and high-trust human interaction that cannot be replicated by AI.

title: Educational support assistants
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with administrative tasks, lesson preparation, and generating personalized learning materials for students with diverse needs, providing moderate productivity gains. However, the core of the role involves physical presence, emotional support, and the management of complex behavioral or special educational needs, which are inherently human-centric and not substitutable by AI.

title: Higher & further education teaching professionals
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these professionals by automating lesson planning, grading, and research synthesis, allowing for more personalized student feedback and higher research output. However, substitution remains low because higher and further education require high-level critical thinking, human mentorship, and the delivery of accredited, trust-based qualifications that AI cannot yet independently provide.

title: Primary & nursery teachers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments teachers by automating lesson planning, grading, and the creation of personalized learning materials for diverse student needs. However, the core of primary and nursery education requires physical presence, emotional intelligence, and the management of social development, which are not substitutable by AI.

title: Secondary teachers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments secondary teachers by automating lesson planning, generating assessment materials, and providing personalized feedback for students. However, substitution remains low due to the essential human requirements of classroom management, social-emotional support, and the regulated nature of the Irish education system.

title: Teaching & other educational professionals
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating lesson planning, generating educational content, and providing personalized feedback for students, which increases teacher throughput. However, the core of the role—particularly for special education and school leadership—requires high levels of human empathy, physical presence, and complex behavioral management that AI cannot replicate.

title: Trainers & instructors, career guidance
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating lesson planning, generating training materials, and providing data-driven career path analysis. However, substitution remains limited because vocational training, driving instruction, and career guidance require high levels of interpersonal trust, physical presence, and nuanced human judgment.

title: Care workers, home carers, etc.
exposure_rationale: The core of this role involves physical presence, manual assistance, and emotional support for an ageing population, which AI cannot replicate. While AI might marginally assist with administrative reporting or monitoring, it cannot perform the essential human-to-human care tasks that define the occupation.

title: Childminders etc.
exposure_rationale: The core of childminding and nursery work is physical presence, emotional intelligence, and the safety-critical supervision of minors, which AI cannot perform. While AI might offer minor assistance in generating activity ideas or tracking developmental milestones, it is largely irrelevant to the primary labor-intensive and trust-based tasks of the role.

title: Other caring services
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of nursing auxiliaries, ambulance staff, and mortuary assistants are heavily physical and require presence in a clinical or sensitive environment. While AI might offer minor assistance in logging patient data or scheduling, it cannot replace the manual care, physical handling, and human empathy that define these roles.

title: Social workers & welfare professionals
exposure_rationale: AI can significantly augment social workers by automating case documentation, summarizing complex histories, and assisting in resource matching for welfare services. However, the core of the role relies on human empathy, ethical judgment, and navigating sensitive interpersonal dynamics, which makes the risk of full substitution very low.

title: Welfare & housing associate professionals
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating case notes, drafting housing reports, and providing data-driven insights for welfare assessments. However, the core of the work involves high-stakes human judgment, crisis intervention, and building trust with vulnerable populations, which makes the role highly resistant to full substitution.

title: Barristers, judges, solicitors & related professionals
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments legal work through automated discovery, case law research, and initial drafting of contracts or pleadings, greatly increasing practitioner throughput. However, substitution remains limited because core functions like courtroom advocacy, judicial decision-making, and high-stakes client advisory rely on regulated human judgment, ethics, and trust-based relationships.

title: Other protective service occupations
exposure_rationale: AI provides moderate productivity gains in administrative reporting, logistics planning, and surveillance analysis for military and emergency services. However, the core functions—physical intervention, emergency response, and the exercise of legal authority—require human presence and regulated judgment, making the role highly resistant to substitution.

title: Police officers (e.g. Gardaí)
exposure_rationale: AI provides moderate complementary benefits through automated report writing, evidence analysis, and predictive policing tools, but the core role remains human-centric. Substitution is extremely low due to the necessity of physical presence, legal authority, complex human judgment in high-stakes situations, and the trust-based nature of community policing.

title: Security guards
exposure_rationale: AI complements security through enhanced surveillance analytics and automated threat detection, but the core role requires physical presence and intervention. Substitution is moderate as automated gates, drones, and AI-driven monitoring can reduce the headcount needed for routine patrolling and access control.

title: Architects & town planners, architectural technologists & surveyors
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through automated BIM modeling, generative design for town planning, and rapid quantity surveying calculations, greatly increasing professional throughput. However, substitution remains limited because the core work requires navigating complex Irish regulatory frameworks, physical site inspections, and high-stakes human negotiation with stakeholders and planning authorities.

title: Bricklayers & plasterers
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of bricklaying and plastering are highly manual, requiring physical dexterity and on-site presence that AI cannot replicate or significantly augment. While digital transformation and 'Modern Methods of Construction' are mentioned, these primarily affect planning and logistics rather than the physical application of materials by these specific trades.

title: Carpenters & joiners
exposure_rationale: The core of the role involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and on-site problem solving that AI cannot replicate or significantly assist. While digital transformation and Modern Methods of Construction (MMC) may introduce some AI-driven planning or off-site precision cutting, the vast majority of the work remains hands-on and non-information based.

title: Civil engineers & construction project managers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through BIM optimization, automated structural analysis, and complex project scheduling, greatly increasing professional throughput. However, substitution remains low because the core work requires physical site inspections, regulatory accountability, and high-stakes human judgment in safety-critical environments.

title: Construction operatives & elementary
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of scaffolders, road, and rail operatives are almost entirely physical and require presence in dynamic, outdoor environments where AI cannot currently operate. While digital transformation and 'modern methods of construction' may introduce some data-driven scheduling or advanced machinery, the manual labor and safety-critical physical coordination remain beyond the reach of AI substitution or significant augmentation.

title: Construction related technicians
exposure_rationale: AI and BIM (Building Information Modelling) tools significantly augment technicians by automating complex calculations, 3D modeling, and regulatory compliance checks, greatly increasing their productivity. While AI can automate routine draughting and basic CAD tasks (substitutable), the role still requires human oversight for site-specific problem solving, physical site inspections, and cross-disciplinary collaboration.

title: Other construction trades
exposure_rationale: These roles involve physical labor, manual dexterity, and on-site problem solving in unpredictable environments, which are currently beyond AI capabilities. While digital transformation and BIM may slightly assist supervisors with scheduling or logistics, the core tasks of roofing, glazing, and steel erection remain entirely human-led and physical.

title: Painters & decorators
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of a painter and decorator are physical and manual, requiring on-site presence and dexterity that current AI cannot replicate or significantly augment. While AI might offer minor assistance in administrative scheduling or color visualization for clients, it does not materially increase the throughput or quality of the physical labor involved.

title: Plumbers
outlook_desc: Carpenters In 2024, at 771, the number of new carpentry and joinery apprentice registrations was 8%
exposure_rationale: The core of plumbing is a manual, physical trade requiring on-site presence, tactile dexterity, and navigation of unique physical environments that AI cannot replicate. While AI might marginally assist with administrative scheduling or basic diagnostic troubleshooting via a mobile app, it cannot perform the physical installation of heat pumps or repair of water systems.

title: Butchers, fishmongers, bakers etc.
exposure_rationale: These roles are primarily defined by manual dexterity, physical presence, and sensory judgment in food preparation, which AI cannot perform. While AI might offer minor assistance in inventory management or recipe optimization, the core tasks of de-boning, butchery, and artisanal baking remain highly resistant to both augmentation and substitution by current AI technologies.

title: Electrical & electronic trades etc.
exposure_rationale: The core of these roles involves physical installation, maintenance, and repair of electrical systems and infrastructure, which AI cannot perform. AI complements the role through digital diagnostics, reading complex technical drawings on digital devices, and managing programmable logic controllers (PLCs), but the physical and safety-critical nature of the work prevents substitution.

title: Other metal-related trades
exposure_rationale: While AI can augment CNC programming, CAD design, and diagnostic troubleshooting for refrigeration and precision instruments, the core work is physical, involving manual dexterity and on-site problem-solving. Substitution is low because these trades require physical presence and the manipulation of hardware in non-standardized environments, which current AI cannot perform without advanced robotics.

title: Other skilled trades
exposure_rationale: These roles are primarily physical and manual, requiring on-site presence for tasks like welding, furniture making, and electrical installation, which limits AI's ability to substitute for the core work. Complementary exposure is low but present through AI-assisted CAD/digital drawings and the management of complex automated systems (e.g., CNC and PLC programming) mentioned in the report.

title: Vehicle trades
exposure_rationale: AI complements these roles through advanced diagnostics, digital drawings, and predictive maintenance schedules, particularly as vehicles become more digitized (EVs). However, the core economic activity is highly manual, requiring physical dexterity and on-site presence to repair and assemble hardware, making substitution by AI virtually impossible.

title: Welding and fabricators
exposure_rationale: AI provides modest augmentation through digital drawing interpretation, CAD/CAM integration, and optimizing CNC programming for fabricators. However, the core work is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, on-site problem solving, and specialized certifications (TIG/ARC) that remain resistant to AI substitution, though traditional automation (robotics) continues to impact high-volume factory welding.

title: Animal carers
exposure_rationale: Veterinarians and nurses benefit from AI in diagnostic imaging and medical research, but the core of the role involves physical handling, surgery, and emotional client interaction. For other roles like zookeepers and groomers, the work is almost entirely manual and sensory, making substitution by AI virtually impossible.

title: Elementary agriculture occupations
exposure_rationale: The core tasks are physical and manual, such as harvesting, animal handling, and forestry work, which offer limited scope for AI-driven productivity augmentation. While the report mentions automated processes (like robotic milking or automated harvesting) could reduce labor demand, these are primarily hardware-driven robotics rather than software-based AI substitution.

title: Farmers
exposure_rationale: AI offers moderate complementary value through precision agriculture, such as optimizing fertilizer use and climate adaptation strategies mentioned in the report. However, the core of the role remains highly physical and tied to land management, making direct substitution by AI alone very low, even if automated machinery (robotics) handles specific tasks.

title: Horticultural, agricultural & fishing trades n.e.c.
exposure_rationale: These roles are primarily defined by physical labor, outdoor presence, and manual dexterity in variable environments, which limits direct AI substitution. Complementary exposure is modest, involving AI-driven optimization for soil health, crop management, or biodiversity tracking, but the core tasks remain hands-on.

title: Managers in horticulture, agriculture & fishing
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these managers through precision agriculture, climate modeling, and resource optimization, which is critical given the 86% third-level education rate and the need to adapt to climate targets. However, substitution remains low because the role requires physical site management, complex human judgment in unpredictable biological environments, and the oversight of physical labor and livestock.

title: Bar staff
exposure_rationale: The core of the role involves physical dexterity, social interaction, and regulated service of alcohol, which AI cannot perform. While AI might marginally assist with inventory management or automated ordering systems, the high-trust and physical nature of the work makes it largely resistant to both augmentation and substitution.

title: Catering & bar managers
exposure_rationale: AI can significantly augment administrative tasks like inventory management, staff scheduling, and financial reporting, but the core of the role involves physical presence, staff leadership, and managing human customer experiences. Substitution is low because the role requires real-time problem solving and trust-based oversight in a physical hospitality environment that cannot be automated by current AI.

title: Chefs & cooks
exposure_rationale: The core of the role involves physical food preparation, sensory judgment, and manual dexterity in a high-pressure environment, which AI cannot perform. While AI can marginally assist with menu planning, inventory management, or recipe scaling (complementary), the physical nature of the work and the high value placed on human culinary skill make substitution extremely unlikely.

title: Hospitality managers
exposure_rationale: AI can significantly augment management tasks such as dynamic pricing, inventory forecasting, and staff scheduling, which improves operational efficiency. However, the core of the role involves physical presence, crisis management, and high-touch human interaction with guests and staff, making the role difficult to automate entirely.

title: Housekeepers etc.
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of housekeeping are physical and manual, requiring presence in a physical environment that current AI and robotics cannot yet navigate or manipulate cost-effectively. While managers may see minor productivity gains from AI-driven scheduling or inventory software, the vast majority of the role remains untouched by AI augmentation or substitution.

title: Kitchen & catering assistants
exposure_rationale: The role is primarily defined by physical labor, manual dexterity, and presence in a kitchen environment, offering almost no opportunity for AI-driven productivity augmentation. While specialized robotics exist for food prep, they are not AI-software substitutions, and the high level of job churn and low-cost labor in this sector makes the economic case for AI replacement currently negligible.

title: Waiters & waitresses
exposure_rationale: The role is primarily defined by physical presence, manual dexterity, and human-to-human service, which limits AI's ability to augment or replace the core worker. While AI can substitute for routine tasks like order-taking via kiosks or QR codes, the high share of youth employment and the social nature of the hospitality experience in Ireland maintain a strong requirement for human staff.

title: Artistic, literary & media associate professionals
exposure_rationale: This group has high complementary exposure as AI tools significantly accelerate creative workflows for graphic designers, writers, and animators through rapid prototyping and drafting. However, substitutable exposure is also high because commodity-level content creation, translation, and basic design are increasingly automated by generative AI, though high-end artistic performance and human-centric roles like acting and directing remain more resilient.

title: Hairdressers & beauticians, etc.
exposure_rationale: The core economic activity of this role is physical, manual labor requiring high dexterity and human presence, which AI cannot perform. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like appointment scheduling or social media marketing, it does not augment the primary service of cutting hair or performing beauty treatments.

title: Leisure & travel service occupations
exposure_rationale: Travel agents and air travel assistants face high substitution as AI-driven booking engines and automated customer service handle routine logistics. Conversely, sports assistants and leisure attendants have low exposure due to the physical and safety-critical nature of their work (e.g., lifeguards, camp counselors), though they may see minor productivity gains in scheduling and program planning.

title: Media profs. & librarians
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating research, drafting press releases, and generating creative concepts for advertising and journalism. However, while routine content and data curation are highly substitutable, the core requirements of high-level editorial judgment, public relations strategy, and the physical management of cultural archives maintain a need for human oversight.

title: Sports & fitness occupations
exposure_rationale: AI complements these roles through personalized training data analysis, automated workout planning, and performance tracking, but the core value remains human motivation and physical presence. Substitution risk is low because while digital platforms can deliver content, they cannot replace the physical supervision, safety monitoring, and interpersonal coaching required in sports and fitness environments.

title: Aircraft pilots, ship officers, air traffic controllers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through advanced route optimization, fuel efficiency modeling, and predictive maintenance alerts, enhancing safety and throughput. However, substitution remains low due to the extreme safety-critical nature of the work, the requirement for human accountability in emergencies, and the high level of regulatory and public trust required for autonomous transport of passengers and cargo.

title: Large goods vehicle drivers
exposure_rationale: AI provides modest complementary benefits through optimized route planning and fuel efficiency monitoring, but the core task remains physical operation. While autonomous vehicle technology represents a long-term substitutable threat, current regulatory, infrastructure, and safety requirements in the Irish context keep immediate substitution very low.

title: Managers & directors in transport & logistics
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through advanced route planning, supply chain optimization, and predictive analytics for warehouse management. While routine logistics coordination and data-heavy planning are susceptible to automation, the core managerial functions—such as navigating geopolitical supply chain disruptions, managing personnel, and high-level strategic decision-making—require human judgment and accountability.

title: Mobile machine drivers & operatives
exposure_rationale: AI and automation offer moderate substitution potential through autonomous forklifts and precision agricultural machinery, though complex environments still require human oversight. Complementary exposure is limited to route optimization and sensor-based safety alerts, as the core task remains physical operation rather than information processing.

title: Other drivers & transport operatives
exposure_rationale: AI and automation provide modest complementary benefits through route optimization and real-time traffic management for drivers. While autonomous vehicle technology represents a high theoretical substitution risk, the current regulatory environment, safety requirements, and the need for human oversight in complex urban or marine environments keep immediate displacement moderate.

title: Stock control, transport & distribution administrative occupations
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through automated route optimization, predictive inventory management, and demand forecasting, increasing the throughput of coordinators. However, the core administrative tasks of data entry, stock tracking, and routine logistics documentation are highly susceptible to full automation by integrated ERP systems and AI-driven supply chain software.

title: Taxi/cab drivers & chauffeurs
exposure_rationale: AI provides modest complementary benefits through optimized route planning and real-time traffic management, but the core task is physical. While autonomous vehicle technology represents a long-term substitutable threat, current regulatory, safety, and infrastructure constraints in Ireland keep immediate substitution risk very low.

title: Elementary administrative
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of mail sorting, message routing, and elementary data entry are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and OCR technologies, leading to high substitutable exposure. There is limited room for productivity augmentation (complementary exposure) as these roles involve routine physical or digital processing rather than complex decision-making or creative output.

title: Government administrative occupations
exposure_rationale: AI can significantly augment these roles by drafting policy documents, summarizing public consultations, and managing complex scheduling, which increases individual productivity. However, a high volume of core tasks—such as routine data processing, application vetting, and basic report generation—is highly substitutable, as noted by the sector's ongoing shift toward digitalization and the reduction of routine tasks.

title: Office managers & supervisors administrative occupations
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments office managers by automating scheduling, drafting communications, and synthesizing reports, allowing them to focus on higher-level coordination. However, while routine administrative supervision is highly substitutable, the core of the role involves human-centric management, conflict resolution, and physical office oversight which limits full displacement.

title: Other administrators
exposure_rationale: These roles involve high volumes of routine data processing, record keeping, and scheduling that are highly susceptible to AI automation and digital transformation. While AI can significantly augment the productivity of remaining staff in managing complex HR or sales workflows, the core economic activity of many 'records' and 'clerk' roles is increasingly replaceable by autonomous software agents.

title: PAs & other secretaries, etc.
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments PAs by automating scheduling, drafting complex correspondence, and summarizing meetings, which increases their throughput. However, the core economic activities of typists and general secretaries—such as transcription, routine document preparation, and basic coordination—are highly substitutable by AI, as noted by the report's mention of digitalization reducing demand for these roles.

title: Receptionists
exposure_rationale: AI can significantly automate core tasks like call routing, appointment scheduling, and basic inquiry handling, which the SOLAS report notes is already reducing demand. While AI complements the role by assisting with email drafting and information retrieval, the high substitutability reflects that many front-desk functions can now be handled by autonomous digital kiosks and AI voice agents.

title: Advertising, marketing & sales directors
exposure_rationale: Directors in these fields benefit significantly from AI-driven data analytics, market trend forecasting, and automated campaign optimization, which enhances their strategic decision-making and productivity. However, their core responsibilities involve high-level leadership, complex stakeholder management, and brand trust, which are deeply human-centric and difficult for AI to replace autonomously.

title: Business sales executives
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles by automating lead generation, personalising outreach, and providing data-driven insights for account management. However, the core of the role relies on human-to-human negotiation, relationship building, and complex trust-based interactions that are difficult for AI to fully replace in a B2B context.

title: Customer service occupations
exposure_rationale: While AI can augment customer service staff with real-time sentiment analysis and suggested responses, the core economic activity of first-line triage and routine query resolution is highly susceptible to full automation via LLM-based agents. The high churn and routine nature of the SOC groups listed (telephonists, market research interviewers) make them prime candidates for displacement, though managers and supervisors retain a higher complementary score for oversight.

title: Managers & directors in retail & wholesale
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through data-driven demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and automated staff scheduling, allowing managers to focus on strategic growth. While routine administrative oversight and basic reporting are substitutable, the core responsibilities of physical site management, human leadership, and navigating complex consumer sentiment require human judgment and presence.

title: Other sales & marketing associate professionals
exposure_rationale: This group includes marketing associates and procurement officers who benefit significantly from AI in data analysis, market research, and drafting, leading to high complementary scores. However, a moderate substitutable risk exists for routine tasks like basic digital marketing execution, inventory procurement, and property listing management which can be increasingly automated.

title: Sales accounts & business development managers
exposure_rationale: AI significantly augments these roles through lead generation, CRM automation, and data-driven customer insights, allowing managers to handle larger portfolios and more complex accounts. However, the core of the role relies on high-stakes negotiation, relationship building, and trust, which are deeply human-centric and difficult for AI to substitute entirely.

title: Sales assistants
exposure_rationale: While AI can complement sales assistants through real-time inventory lookups and personalized product recommendations, the core tasks of checkout operations and basic customer inquiries are highly substitutable by automated systems and AI-driven kiosks. The high volume of routine transactions and the low requirement for third-level qualifications make this role vulnerable to displacement, as noted in the DETE research cited in the report.

title: Sales related occupations
exposure_rationale: This group includes call centre and sales-related roles that are highly susceptible to automation via AI-driven chatbots and automated collection systems. While AI can complement supervisors and merchandisers with data analytics, the core tasks of many roles in this specific cluster (e.g., debt collection, routine customer service, and basic sales) are increasingly being replaced by autonomous software.

title: Assemblers & routine operatives
exposure_rationale: AI and computer vision can augment routine inspectors and testers by flagging defects for human review, but the physical nature of assembly and fitting limits overall augmentation. While physical robotics is the primary threat to manual assembly, AI-driven automation increasingly handles the sorting, grading, and quality control tasks that previously required human judgment, leading to moderate substitutable exposure.

title: Chemical & related process
exposure_rationale: AI and advanced analytics can complement these operatives by optimizing chemical yields and monitoring safety protocols in real-time, particularly given that 40% of the workforce is third-level educated. However, substitutable exposure remains moderate because the core role involves physical handling and specialized machinery operation in industrial environments that require robotics rather than just AI software to replace.

title: Cleaning
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of window cleaning, street sweeping, and vehicle valeting are physical and manual, offering almost no opportunity for AI-driven productivity augmentation. While specialized robotics (e.g., window-cleaning drones) exist, the current and near-future AI software impact on these elementary roles is negligible as they require physical presence and dexterity in unstructured environments.

title: Food, drink & tobacco operatives
exposure_rationale: The core tasks involve physical labor, manual dexterity, and sensory inspection in environments like meat processing and abattoirs, which are resistant to current AI-driven automation. While digitalization may offer minor productivity gains in monitoring or reporting, the role's primary economic value is physical rather than information-based, making both augmentation and substitution by AI (as opposed to traditional robotics) very low.

title: Other process operatives & process plant occupations
exposure_rationale: These roles are primarily physical and manual, involving the handling of materials and machinery in industrial settings where AI has limited direct utility for augmentation. While robotics and traditional automation pose a significant threat to these routine tasks, the specific impact of AI (generative or cognitive) is lower than in office-based roles, as the core work requires physical presence and dexterity rather than information processing.

title: Plant & machine operatives
exposure_rationale: The core tasks involve physical manipulation of machinery, quarrying, and plant operation which AI cannot perform without advanced robotics, keeping substitution relatively low for now. Complementary exposure is limited as these roles have low information-processing requirements, though AI may marginally assist in monitoring plant diagnostics or energy output optimization.

title: Sales, storage & services
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of shelf filling, hospital portering, and warehouse storage are primarily physical and require presence in a non-structured environment, limiting AI's ability to augment or replace the human worker directly. While AI-driven inventory systems and robotics provide some substitution pressure in large-scale logistics, the 'elementary' nature of these specific roles often involves manual dexterity and human navigation that current AI cannot yet replicate. Complementary exposure is low as these workers have minimal information-processing tasks that could be accelerated by AI tools.
